findings.comment: ZeroDivisionError when nums is empty: len([]) == 0, so this division crashes unconditionally on an empty input. There is no guard (e.g. `if not nums: raise ValueError(...)` or `return 0.0`) before this line.

pct is unconstrained: when pct > 100, price * pct / 100 > price, so the function returns a negative number (the caller is paid to take the item). When pct < 0 the function silently adds to the price instead of discounting it. Neither case raises an error, so misuse produces a silently wrong result.
